title: Diagnose poor multicore scaling
description: When adding threads barely helps, isolate the cause among false sharing, lock contention, memory bandwidth, and NUMA.
body: Steps: 1. Plot throughput vs thread count to see where it flattens. 2. Check for false sharing (perf c2c / HITM events) on hot shared lines. 3. Check lock contention (off-CPU / futex wait time) and memory bandwidth saturation. 4. On multi-socket, check remote-access ratio with numastat. 5. Fix the dominant cause first, then re-measure scaling. Rollback point: before restructuring shared data layout. Validation: throughput scales closer to linear and the implicated counter (HITM, bandwidth, remote access) drops; if scaling is bandwidth-bound, more threads cannot help and the fix must reduce traffic, not add parallelism.
